MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT J-M MANUFACTURING CO, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: David J. Kestenbaum of Walsworth Franklin Bevins & McCall, LLP (415.781.7072) for defendant and moving party; Jennifer Alesio of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. As to the causation issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to present sufficient evidence to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration, defendant's responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Mr. Reichert would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing pipe attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibit I are sustained and to Exhibit J are overruled. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and to the deposition testimony of Mr. Reichert are overruled. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiff's claims against it. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of Aiken Welch, CSR 11596 (510.421.1580) (503/TLJ)
